summary: Add lib.is_terminal_state(state) -> bool to the engine's lib.py (returns True for 'done', 'failed', 'blocked'; False for everything else including None/non-strings). One task: TDD — write tests, watch them fail, add the single-expression function, verify all tests pass, commit. No call-site refactoring, no CLI subcommand, no new deps.

ledger.what: New dedicated test module `tests/test_lib_is_terminal_state.py` rather than co-locating in an existing module
ledger.why: The issue says 'focused pytest unit tests'; existing test modules are named per concept (test_decide.py for decide(), test_correlation.py for match_run_by_cid, etc.); a dedicated module follows the established repo convention and keeps the new tests easily discoverable.
ledger.what_i_did: Created a new `tests/test_lib_is_terminal_state.py` module in the plan; it imports lib the same way test_decide.py and test_correlation.py do (sys.path + ENGINE path prefix).

blast_radius.why: A new file only; zero impact on existing tests or production code.

reversibility.why: Merging the tests into another module is a trivial git move/edit; no downstream consumers depend on this file's name.

ledger.revisit_if: Implementer prefers co-locating with an existing lib-unit test module such as test_correlation.py.

ledger.what: The three terminal states are exactly `"done"`, `"failed"`, `"blocked"` — no others exist or are planned
ledger.why: The issue specifies them explicitly. PHASE_LABEL_DEFAULTS at lib.py:586-591 confirms these are the engine-level terminal head keys; 'setup' is the only other entry and is a transient initial state. Usage in advance.py and next.py corroborates: done/failed/blocked are the only values written as final process states.
ledger.what_i_did: Verified in .github/agent-factory/engine/lib.py lines 586-591 (PHASE_LABEL_DEFAULTS) and cross-checked against advance.py and next.py grep output showing done/failed/blocked as the only written final states.

blast_radius.why: Defines the function's exact contract. Any future terminal-state extension would need to update this set, but that is a trivial one-line change with no cascading effects.

reversibility.why: Adding a fourth terminal state is a one-line set expansion in the function body; the function has no side effects and no downstream callers to update in this change.

ledger.revisit_if: A new terminal state (beyond done/failed/blocked) is added to the engine protocol.

ledger.why: The issue scopes the change to 'add the helper + tests only'; the CLI in _cli() is for orchestrator shell use; no workflow currently needs to call this predicate from a shell run: step; adding an unneeded subcommand would be YAGNI.
ledger.what_i_did: Omitted from _cli(); the function is library-only (callers import lib and call lib.is_terminal_state()).

blast_radius.why: The CLI is not changed; existing orchestrator steps are unaffected.

reversibility.why: Adding a subcommand later is a purely additive change; no existing code would need modification.

ledger.revisit_if: An orchestrator workflow step needs to call is_terminal_state from a shell run: block.
